english: a demon Glr.; even madness may be attributed to the heart Do. - snyiṅ-nas (snying nas) 1. heartily, zealously, earnestly, e.g. looking for or to a thing Dzl.; snyiṅ ṭág-pa-nas (snying thag pa nas) with all one's heart, most earnestly, devoutly, e.g. to say one's prayers Thgy. 2. actually, really, koṅ snyiṅ-nas mi °byíṅ-ba yin (khong snying nas mi 'bying ba yin) really he does not sink! (the water actually bears him) Mil. 3. v. snyiṅ-po (snying po). Comp. and deriv. snyiṅ-ku (snying khu) v. nyiṅ-ḱu (nying khu). - snyiṅ-k̇áms (snying khams) courage Sch. - *nyiṅ-ṭág k̇ól* (*nying thag khol*) W. my heart's blood is boiling (with anger etc.) - snyiṅ-dgá (snying dga') v. above. - snyiṅ-čan (snying can) courageous, spirited Ld. - *nyiṅ-ču žug* (*nying chu zhug*) W. afflicted with dropsy in the pericardium, hydrocardia. - snyiṅ-rje (snying rje), resp. tugs-rje (thugs rje) (करुणा) kindness, mercy, compassion, mi-la snyiṅ-rje sgóm-pa (mi la snying rje sgom pa) to commiserate, to pity a person Mil.; snyiṅ-rjes kyáb-pa (snying rjes khyab pa) id. with respect to a great number of beings, to embrace with affection Dzl.; snyiṅ-rjes nón-te (snying rjes non te) overpowered by compassion; *nyiṅ-že tsór-če* (*nying zhe tshor ce*) W. to have compassion; snyiṅ-rje-čan (snying rje can), snyiṅ-rje daṅ ldán-pa (snying rje dang ldan pa) compassionate, merciful Dzl.: snyiṅ-rje-skad (snying rje skad) lamentation, a cry of compassion Dzl.; snyiṅ-rje-mo: (snying rje mo) 1. k̇yod snyiṅ-rje-mo raṅ žig °dug (khyod snying rje mo rang zhig 'dug) you are much to be pitied Mil. 2. col.: dearest, most beloved, amiable, charming; also snyiṅ-rje (snying rje) for snyiṅ-rje-mo (snying rje mo), snyiṅ-rje mdzá-bo (snying rje mdza' bo) my poor little friend. - snyiṅ-nyé-ba (snying nye ba), col. *nyiṅ nyé-mo* (*nying nye mo*), friendly, amicable, loving, affectionate; friend; friendship, snyiṅ-nye bú-mo (snying nye bu mo) a woman connected by friendship with, a woman, the friend of (a sick person mentioned) Lt. - snyiṅ-ytam (snying gtam) a confidential speaking, for exhortation, consolation, or encouragement; brtsé-bai snyiṅ-ytam (brtse ba'i snying gtam) affectionate exhortation Glr.; pán-pai snyiṅ-ytam (phan pa'i snying gtam) useful admonition etc. Mil. - snyiṅ-stobs (snying stobs) courage. - snyiṅ-°dód-pa (snying 'dod pa) to wish, to desire, to long for, za-snyiṅ-°dód-pa (za snying 'dod pa) to wish to eat, to be craving for food Thgy.; °gro-snyiṅ-°dód-pa ('gro snying 'dod pa) to wish to go. - snyiṅ-rdúṅ-ba (snying rdung ba) palpitation of heart Sch. - *nyiṅ daṅ* (*nying dang*) (etymol. dubious) *čó-če* (*co ce*) W. ccd. to interest one's self for, to take an interest in. - snyiṅ-sdúg (snying sdug) W. liked, beloved; darling, favourite, e.g. a child; nyiṅ-dúg šig dug* (*nying dug shig dug*) W. he is a general favourite; *ṅa °di nyiṅ-dúg čó dug* (*nga 'di nying dug co dug*) W. I am very fond of this, it is my favourite (pursuit etc.); but snyiṅ-ma-sdúg (snying ma sdug) bad people Mil. - snyiṅ ná-ba (snying na ba) 1. = snyiṅ-nád (snying nad). 2. 'heart-sickness', grief, on account of injury suffered from others, curable only by indemnity paid or revenge taken. - snyiṅ-nád (snying nad) disease of the heart. - snyiṅ-po (snying po) (सार, गर्भ) the chief part, main substance, quintessence, e.g. the cream of the milk Med.; the soft part of a loaf, the wick of a lamp Dzl.; frq. fig.: the main substance of a doctrine, a book etc., don-snyiṅ byín-pa (don snying byin pa) to give a summary, the sum and substance (of a writing); séms-čan tams-čád saṅs-rgyás-kyi snyiṅ-po-čan yín-na (sems can thams cad sangs rgyas kyi snying po can yin na) if all beings have the pith and essence of the nature of Buddha in themselves Thgy. 5, 8; the Ommanipadmehūm is called the snyiṅ-po (snying po) of religion Glr.; snyiṅ-po-méd-pa (snying po med pa) worthless, null, void, snyiṅ-pos dbén-pa (snying pos dben pa) id. Tar. 185, 2; de-bžin-gšégs-pai snyiṅ-po (de bzhin gshegs pa'i snying po) the spirit of Tathāgata Was.; snyiṅ-po-byaṅ-čúb- (snying po byang chub) (or byaṅ-čúb-snyiṅ-po (byang chub snying po)) -la mčís-pa (la mchis pa) to become Buddha Thgy.; srog(-gi) snyiṅ(-po) °búl-ba (srog gi snying po 'bul ba) Mil. frq. to offer one's heart's blood, to pledge one's own life. - snyiṅ-rtsá (snying rtsa) (col.) the great veins connected with the heart, perh. = snyiṅ-luṅ (snying lung). - snyiṅ-rtse (snying rtse) the tip or apex of the heart, mentioned by Mil. as a particular dainty (perh. only by way of a jest). - snyiṅ-brtse-ba (snying brtse ba), resp. túgs-brtsé-ba (thugs brtse ba), vb., also sbst. and adj., not much differing from snyiṅ-rje (snying rje): love, pity etc. frq.; Dzl.: bú-la snyiṅ-brtse-nas (bu la snying brtse nas); tams-čád-la snyiṅ-brtse-ba yín-na (thams cad la snying brtse ba yin na); de-dag-la snyiṅ-brtse-bai pyír (de dag la snying brtse ba'i phyir); snyiṅ-brtse-bai sems skyés-te (snying brtse ba'i sems skyes te) etc. - snyiṅ-tsím (snying tshim) contentment, satisfaction, sometimes also pleasure felt at the misfortune of others Pth., snyiṅ-tsím °débs-pa (snying tshim 'debs pa) to manifest such an enjoyment. - snyiṅ-tsil (snying tshil) the fat about the heart Cs. - snyiṅ-žo-ša (snying zho sha) v. žó-ša (zho sha). - snyiṅ-rús (snying rus), resp. tugs-rús (thugs rus) (acc. to Mil.: snyiṅ-gi rús-pa tón-par gyis (snying gi rus pa thon par gyis) let energy and diligence arise in you); firmness of mind (heart) i.e. 1. diligence, zeal, perseverance
